description: Court record. Location: Fustat. Dated: last decade of Shevaṭ 1459 Seleucid = January 1148 CE. The cantor Bū l-Ḥasan b. Naḥman confesses to have beaten and cursed his wife Naẓar bt. ʿAmram and to have falsely accused her of filching money and utensils from his house. The wife ran away from the house as a result. In the present settlement, Bū l-Ḥasan forgoes all his claims against his wife and promises not to beat or curse her, under penalty to divorce her and to pay in cash the sums due to her under her marriage contract, in case he relapses. Written and witnessed by Natan b. Shelomo ha-Kohen. Recto is blank. (Information mainly from Goitein notes and index card linked below and Goitein, Med Soc, Vol. 3, pp. 187–88.)
description_he: רשומת בית דין; פוסטאט; סוף שבט 1459 לספירת השטרות (ינואר 1148 לספירה); החזן בו אל-חסן בן נחמן מודה שהכה וקילל את אשתו נצר בת עמרם ומתחייב שלא לחזור על כך
